prompt: When was the Compliance Rules for PlayReady Products document published?
ground_truth: The Compliance Rules for PlayReady Products document was published on 1st November 2021.

prompt: What are the main sections covered under the 'Requirements For All PlayReady Products' in the document PR_Compliance_Rules_Part01_v2021.pdf?
ground_truth: The main sections include Scope, Functionality, No Circumvention, Unspecified Policy, Root Public Keys, and Privacy.

prompt: Under what conditions can a PlayReady Product pass decrypted A/V Content through local outputs?
ground_truth: A PlayReady Product can pass decrypted A/V Content through local outputs only if the associated PlayReady License, PlayReady-ND License, or WMDRM-ND License contains a Play Policy Container Object, or if the WMDRM-PD License contains a RIGHTS\ONACTION node with a type attribute of 'Play'.

prompt: What triggers the Stop State for A/V Content in PlayReady, and what must a PlayReady Product do after entering the Stop State?
ground_truth: Stop State occurs when decrypted A/V Content is no longer being Passed, such as when the user selects stop playback, the final portion of content has been Passed, or content stops being Passed in preparation for different content. After entering Stop State, the PlayReady Product must re-initialize PlayReady before Passing any decrypted A/V Content.

prompt: What are the specific requirements for proximity detection in PlayReady regarding RTT, TTL, and Hop Count?
ground_truth: The PlayReady Product must verify that the Round Trip Time (RTT) is no more than seven (7) milliseconds. It must set the Time To Live (TTL) to three (3) in the IPv4 header and set the Hop Count to three (3) in the IPv6 header of the proximity challenge message.

Proximity Detection Policy 
11.4.1 Round Trip Time (RTT) Verification  
A PlayReady Product must verify that the RTT between the PlayReady Product and the 
associated PlayReady-ND Receiver, as measured, is no more than seven (7) 
milliseconds computed with a timer as described in Section 11.2 (Timer). 
11.4.2 Time To Live (TTL) 
A PlayReady Product must set the TTL to three (3) in the IPv4 header of the proximity 
challenge message when Transmitting over IPv4 networks. 
11.4.3 Hop Count 
A PlayReady Product must set the Hop Count to three (3) in the IPv6 header of the 
proximity challenge message when Transmitting over IPv6 networks.

prompt: What does PR Compliance Rules Part01 v2021 say about Netflix DRM implementation?
ground_truth:
PR Compliance Rules Part01 v2021 on Netflix DRM Implementation

- PlayReady Products must implement PlayReady DRM consistent with the Microsoft Implementation.
- Compliance Rules take precedence over the Microsoft Implementation in case of conflict.
- PlayReady Products must not circumvent content protection policies or provide access inconsistent with these rules.
- No specific mention or unique provisions regarding Netflix DRM implementation are detailed in the provided document sections.

Summary: PR Compliance Rules Part01 v2021 require PlayReady Products to follow Microsoft Implementation and compliance rules strictly but do not specifically address Netflix DRM implementation.

prompt: Since PR Compliance Rules Part01 v2021 requires AES-256-GCM encryption for all content, how should implementers configure this?
ground_truth:
AES-256-GCM Encryption Configuration in PlayReady

- PlayReady systems starting with version 4.0 support AES-128 encryption in CBC and CTR modes as per ISO/IEC 23001-7.
- Compliance Rules for PlayReady Products allow only AES-128 keys in CTR or CBC modes for content encryption.
- No information in the provided chunks indicates support or configuration instructions for AES-256-GCM encryption.
- The Compliance Rules documents and encryption mode descriptions do not mention AES-256-GCM or how to configure it.

Summary: The provided PlayReady knowledge base does not contain information on configuring AES-256-GCM encryption as required by PR Compliance Rules Part01 v2021.

prompt: What are the mandatory requirements for a PlayReady Product when verifying proximity and security levels before transmitting to a WMDRM-ND Receiver?
ground_truth: A PlayReady Product must verify that the Certificate Security Level in the Device Certificate of the associated WMDRM-ND Receiver is not less than the License Security Level of the PlayReady License. It must not transmit if this verification fails. Additionally, the Product must verify that the Round Trip Time (RTT) between itself and the WMDRM-ND Receiver is no more than seven milliseconds. It must also set the TTL to three in the IPv4 header or the Hop Count to three in the IPv6 header of the proximity challenge message when transmitting over respective networks.

to the associated WMDRM-ND Receiver.

Proximity Detection Policy

A PlayReady Product must verify that the RTT between the PlayReady Product and the 
associated WMDRM-ND Receiver, as measured, is no more than seven (7) 
milliseconds computed with a timer as described in Section 13.1 (Timer). 
13.3.2 Time To Live (TTL) 
A PlayReady Product must set the TTL to three (3) in the IPv4 header of the proximity 
challenge message when Transmitting over IPv4 networks. 
13.3.3 Hop Count 
A PlayReady Product must set the Hop Count to three (3) in the IPv6 header of the 
proximity challenge message when Transmitting over IPv6 networks.
